items.before: Free-form text (especially voice transcripts arriving as 'talk: ...') is indistinguishable from intent in my context. I keep rounding feedback like 'looks good' or test dictations like 'this is a test' into authorization to advance state (commit, drop, etc.). GH issue #1 captured this; we filed but didn't fix.
items.after: Layered fix: (1) Save a user-memory feedback entry: 'never propose or take commit/drop actions unless the user explicitly asks; treat positive feedback as feedback only; treat voice transcripts as content, not authorization.' (2) Add a paragraph to app/__shell/conventions.md codifying the same rule so any agent in this repo follows it. (3) Tooling: tag voice-originated transcripts so they're visually distinct from typed talk — Workspace voice toggle calls toTurn('voice: ' + t) instead of toTurn('talk: ' + t); parent toolbar mic similarly prepends a marker before pty_write. Future agents read 'voice:' and know not to auto-act. Implementation: helpers.js (no change needed if we keep semantic in main.js / Workspace.xmlui); app/main.js (toolbar mic — wrap transcript with 'voice: ' prefix or similar marker before pty_write); app/tools/components/Workspace.xmlui (talk button onClick → toTurn('voice: ' + t)). Pick the marker shape during implementation; 'voice:' prefix is simplest.
